hypothesis: If the invariance of Li⁺ migration channel size across lithiation states is a reliable predictor of structural resilience in Si–X binary alloys, then extending this descriptor to ternary Si–X–Y systems where X provides electronic stabilization (e.g., a transition metal) and Y provides geometric channel-pinning (e.g., a large-radius metalloid), will yield compounds exhibiting simultaneously lower channel-size variation slopes than any binary Si–X end-member, quantifiably demonstrating that synergistic multi-element sublattice engineering produces a non-additive improvement in channel stability that cannot be predicted by linear interpolation between the binary component descriptors.
